  "decision": "fail",

  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Backstabbed termination on a 20A circuit violates NEC 110.14(A) which requires terminals to be used only for conductors they are rated for; typical backstab connections are rated for 15A only.",
    "Backstab connections are prone to high-resistance faults and are not considered a reliable termination method for industrial environments."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Remove backstabbed connection and terminate conductor under the screw terminal using a proper shepherd's hook or straight insertion per manufacturer instructions.",
    "Verify terminal torque with a calibrated torque screwdriver per manufacturer specifications.",
    "Re-inspect all terminations on the circuit for compliance."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 110.14(A) requires terminations to be made using terminals rated for the conductor and circuit ampacity. Standard backstab (push-in) connections on receptacles are typically rated for 15A circuits only. Using them on a 20A circuit is a code violation and creates a fire risk due to potential overheating. The work is rough-complete but must be corrected before close-out.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 110.14(A)",
    "NEC 110.3(B) (manufacturer instructions)"
  ]
}
